title: Canvas Items, Tags, Layers, Rendering, and Redraw · Behavior Case: Render Layers
summary: Redraw duplicates thousands of line items, solver marks cover walls, stale IDs are mutated, and hit testing scans every object. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render layers increment in tagged layered Canvas render plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.
Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 5355 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; the prior good state must survive failure; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_canvas_rendering_tags_5(expected_items: int = 126, actual_items: int = 126, semantic_tags: int = 5, duplicate_items: int = 0, layer_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0:
        return False, actual_items, "render-count"
    if actual_items != expected_items:
        return False, actual_items, "item-reconciliation"
    if semantic_tags < 4:
        return False, semantic_tags, "tag-contract"
    if duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0:
        return False, duplicate_items + layer_errors, "render-invariant"
    assert actual_items == expected_items
    return True, actual_items, "tagged-render-plan"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Layers model · Theory Render Layers, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Render Layers precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Layers model · Theory Render Layers, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4641 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Layers model · Theory Render Layers, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Creation order will remain obvious as the interface grows.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-render-layers-1vt6rg(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_render_layers_1vt6rg\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actual_items != expected_items)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?semantic_tags < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
